summary: Extend 'todo install-hooks' with optional post-commit auto-sync to Hermes
description:
Once 'todo install-hooks' (child 6784fb63) ships, extend it with an
opt-in `--with-sync` flag (or equivalent) that ALSO installs a
`post-commit` hook calling `todo sync --quiet` for the current repo.

Hook must be best-effort:
- Run in background or with a short timeout so Hermes-down never delays
  the commit user-visibly.
- Swallow all errors. Never exit non-zero — post-commit failures are
  ignored by git but still print scary output if we emit it.
- Only sync when HEAD is on a `todo/*` branch.

`todo install-hooks` itself must support installing OR removing the
post-commit piece independently of the prepare-commit-msg piece, so
users who want commit prefixing without auto-sync (or vice versa) are
served.

Done contract: tests cover (a) post-commit hook calls todo sync only on
todo/* branches, (b) Hermes-unreachable does not block or error visibly,
(c) install/uninstall semantics are independent for the two hooks.

Depends on: 6784fb63 (install-hooks must exist first), e3b282d3 
(todo sync must exist before auto-syncing).
